---
title: "CERP Combined Report"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    self_contained: true
params:
  config_path: !r here::here("render_config.yml")
---

```{r combined-setup, include=FALSE}
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3.00 — Combined report (parent). Stitches the visuals named in the config's
# `combined.include` list into ONE self-contained HTML, in order, under one
# shared house style. It renders NOTHING of its own — each section is a child
# template rendered as an html_fragment with its own data + params, then inlined.
#
# Design notes:
#  * Config (render_config.yml) is the single source of truth; this parent
#    re-reads it so there is no second place to keep in sync.
#  * Each child is rendered in a FRESH R process via xfun::Rscript_call
#    (xfun ships with rmarkdown/knitr — no new dependency). That gives clean
#    param/env isolation and sidesteps nested-knit option clashes.
#  * Children run with combined_mode=TRUE, which (a) suppresses their code echo
#    and (b) skips their per-file CSS injection — the shell below injects
#    cerp_style.css exactly once for the whole page.
#  * A child failure yields an inline error box, not a dead report.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(here)
library(yaml)

`%||%` <- function(x, y) if (is.null(x)) y else x

config   <- yaml::read_yaml(params$config_path)
data_dir <- config$defaults$data_dir %||% "1_data"
combined <- config$combined
reports_by_id <- stats::setNames(
  config$reports,
  vapply(config$reports, function(e) e$id %||% NA_character_, character(1))
)

render_child_fragment <- function(entry) {
  overrides <- entry$params %||% list()
  overrides$combined_mode <- TRUE
  if (!is.null(entry$data)) {
    overrides$data_path <- here::here(data_dir, entry$data)
  }
  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".html")
  # Fresh Rscript process: isolated env, no nested-knit interference.
  xfun::Rscript_call(
    rmarkdown::render,
    list(
      input         = here::here("3_templates", entry$template),
      output_format = rmarkdown::html_fragment(),
      output_dir    = dirname(tmp),
      output_file   = basename(tmp),
      params        = overrides,
      quiet         = TRUE
    )
  )
  paste(readLines(tmp, warn = FALSE), collapse = "\n")
}
```

```{r combined-style, results='asis'}
# House style once for the whole page, plus a little combined-report chrome.
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
cat("
<style>
  .cerp-report { margin: 0 0 3.5rem 0; padding-bottom: 1.5rem;
                 border-bottom: 1px solid #d7e3e6; }
  .cerp-report:last-child { border-bottom: none; }
  .cerp-error  { background: #fbeaea; border: 1px solid #e0b4b4;
                 color: #7a1f1f; padding: 0.9rem 1.1rem; border-radius: 6px;
                 font-family: 'Libre Franklin', sans-serif; }
</style>
")
```

```{r combined-header, results='asis'}
cat(sprintf("<h1>%s</h1>\n", combined$title %||% "CERP Combined Report"))
if (!is.null(combined$author)) {
  cat(sprintf("<p style='color:#5a7079;'>%s &middot; %s</p>\n",
              combined$author, format(Sys.Date(), "%B %d, %Y")))
}
if (!is.null(combined$intro)) {
  cat(sprintf("<p class='lead'>%s</p>\n", combined$intro))
}
```

```{r combined-body, results='asis'}
include_ids <- combined$include %||% list()

if (length(include_ids) == 0) {
  cat("<div class='cerp-error'>combined.include is empty — nothing to stitch.</div>")
}

for (id in include_ids) {
  entry <- reports_by_id[[id]]

  cat(sprintf('\n<div class="cerp-report" id="report-%s">\n', id))

  if (is.null(entry)) {
    cat(sprintf('<div class="cerp-error">Unknown id "%s" in combined.include.</div>', id))
  } else {
    frag <- tryCatch(
      render_child_fragment(entry),
      error = function(e) sprintf(
        '<div class="cerp-error"><strong>Failed to render &quot;%s&quot;:</strong> %s</div>',
        id, conditionMessage(e)
      )
    )
    cat(frag)
  }

  cat('\n</div>\n')
}
```
